name: Tepeyollotl
description: Tepeyollotl, whose name translates to "Heart of the Mountain," is a powerful and complex deity in Aztec mythology. He is the god of earthquakes, echoes, and jaguars, embodying the raw, untamed forces of the earth's interior. He is often depicted as a jaguar leaping towards the sun, representing the earth's energy reaching for the heavens. Tepeyollotl dwells within the mountains, caverns, and the very core of the earth, his roars causing the ground to tremble and echoes to reverberate. He is considered one of the Lords of the Night, ruling over one of the hours and influencing nocturnal events. He is also associated with the eighth day of the Aztec calendar, signifying his importance in the cyclical nature of time and the universe. Some interpretations link him to Tezcatlipoca, suggesting a powerful, unseen force shaping the world. He represents the hidden dangers and unpredictable nature of the earth, demanding respect and caution. He is seen as a protector of certain artisans and is sometimes invoked for strength and courage. His presence is a constant reminder of the earth's immense power and its ability to both nurture and destroy. Offerings were made to appease him and prevent earthquakes, highlighting his crucial role in maintaining cosmic balance. He is a symbol of the primal energy residing within the earth and the connection between the earthly and spiritual realms.
subtitle: Heart of the Mountain, Jaguar God

keyMyths.title: Tepeyollotl's Roar
keyMyths.description: Tepeyollotl's roar is said to cause earthquakes, a reminder of the earth's power. He dwells within the mountains, and his voice resonates throughout the land.

keyMyths.title: The Jaguar's Leap
keyMyths.description: As a jaguar leaping towards the sun, Tepeyollotl embodies the earth's energy reaching for the heavens. This represents his connection to both the terrestrial and celestial realms.

keyMyths.title: Lord of the Night
keyMyths.description: Tepeyollotl rules over one of the hours of the night, influencing nocturnal events and the unseen forces that move in darkness. He is a guardian and a force to be reckoned with.
keyMyths.source: Book of the Gods and Rites

sources.author: H.B. Nicholson
